title: Ethereum Foundation Finalizes Key Upgrades for Glamsterdam
summary: The Ethereum Foundation has set a new gas limit floor and approved an improvement proposal for its upcoming Glamsterdam upgrade. This upgrade is expected to go live in the third quarter of 2026, improving network efficiency and cost.
body:
The Ethereum Foundation has reached significant milestones in its Glamsterdam upgrade, finalizing a new gas limit floor and approving a key improvement proposal. This upgrade, scheduled for the third quarter of 2026, aims to enhance the network's efficiency and reduce transaction costs. The gas limit floor adjustment will help manage network congestion, ensuring smoother transactions for users.

This upgrade matters because it directly impacts how much you pay to use Ethereum. Lower gas fees mean cheaper transactions for everyday users, whether you're transferring funds, using decentralized apps, or trading NFTs. Think of it like setting a speed limit on a busy highway—it keeps things moving smoothly and prevents bottlenecks.

If you use Ethereum regularly, keep an eye out for announcements about the Glamsterdam upgrade. While the exact date isn't set, being aware of these changes can help you plan your transactions around potential network improvements. Stay tuned for more details as the launch approaches.
twitterThread: 🚀 Big news for Ethereum users! The Glamsterdam upgrade is coming, and it could lower your transaction fees. Here's what you need to know:
The Ethereum Foundation just finalized a new gas limit floor and improvement proposal. In plain English: they're tweaking the network to handle more transactions efficiently.
This upgrade is expected to go live in the second half of 2026. The part that matters: lower gas fees mean cheaper transactions for you and me.
Think of it like setting a speed limit on a busy highway. It keeps things moving smoothly and prevents bottlenecks. If you've ever wanted to pay less for Ethereum transactions, this is good news.
Keep an eye out for more details as the launch approaches. If you use Ethereum regularly, this upgrade could save you money.
standaloneTweet: 🚀 Ethereum's Glamsterdam upgrade is coming in late 2026, and it could lower your transaction fees. Here's what you need to know about this big change.
